skill.name: systematic-debugging
skill.description: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes
skill.summary: A structured debugging methodology that teaches AI assistants to find root causes before fixing bugs
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 126 static findings are false positives. The skill contains legitimate debugging methodology with example commands shown as educational content. JavaScript template literals were misidentified as shell execution. Environment variable checks are for NODE_ENV configuration. Keychain commands are debugging examples, not malicious access. No actual security risks found.

low_findings.title: JavaScript Template Literals Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Backticks in TypeScript files are JavaScript template literals for string interpolation, not shell command execution. The static scanner misidentified these as Ruby/shell backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are standard JavaScript template literals using backtick syntax for string interpolation in error messages. No shell or Ruby execution is present.

low_findings.title: Diagnostic Commands Shown as Debugging Examples
low_findings.description: Commands like 'security list-keychains' in SKILL.md are shown as examples of what to check when debugging code signing issues, not actual malicious keychain access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: This is educational content showing users what commands to run when debugging multi-layer system issues. It describes checking keychain state for code signing, a legitimate debugging use case.

low_findings.title: Standard Environment Variable Configuration Pattern
low_findings.description: process.env.NODE_ENV access is a standard, safe pattern for detecting test environments and conditionally running test-specific code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: NODE_ENV is a standard runtime configuration variable. Reading it to check if code is running in test mode is a common, safe pattern used by virtually all Node.js applications.

low_findings.title: Legitimate Test Debugging Scripts
low_findings.description: The find-polluter.sh script uses standard bash command substitution and redirection for running tests and checking file state - a legitimate debugging utility.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: This is a bisection script for finding which test pollutes the environment. Command substitution $(command) and arithmetic $((expr)) are standard bash patterns for legitimate automation.

content.value_statement: Stop wasting time on random fixes. This skill teaches AI assistants a structured debugging methodology that finds root causes before attempting fixes, reducing debugging time from hours to minutes.
actual_capabilities: Guides AI through four-phase debugging process: root cause investigation, pattern analysis, hypothesis testing, and implementation
Teaches how to gather evidence in multi-component systems before proposing fixes
Provides data flow tracing techniques to find the origin of bugs
Helps identify when architectural changes are needed instead of symptom fixes
Includes supporting techniques for condition-based waiting and defense in depth validation
limitations: Does not provide direct code fixes - only guides the investigation process
Requires the AI to have access to the codebase and ability to run tests
May suggest running diagnostic commands that require appropriate system permissions
Does not replace domain expertise for very specialized systems

use_cases.title: Fix Recurring Test Failures
use_cases.description: When tests fail intermittently or consistently, use the systematic approach to trace why rather than applying random fixes that mask the real issue.
use_cases.target_user: Developers working on test suites with flaky or failing tests

use_cases.title: Debug Production Incidents
use_cases.description: When production issues occur, follow the structured investigation to quickly identify root cause rather than guessing at solutions under pressure.
use_cases.target_user: DevOps engineers and developers debugging production issues

use_cases.title: Learn Debugging Discipline
use_cases.description: New developers or AI assistants can learn a proven debugging methodology that prevents common mistakes like premature optimization or symptom-only fixes.
use_cases.target_user: Junior developers and AI assistants learning debugging best practices

prompt_templates.title: Basic Debug Request
prompt_templates.prompt: I'm seeing this error: [paste error]. Please use systematic debugging to investigate the root cause before proposing any fixes.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you encounter a bug or error and want structured investigation

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Component Debugging
prompt_templates.prompt: We have a CI pipeline that signs, and deploys. The builds, signing step is failing. Use systematic debugging to trace which layer is causing the issue.
prompt_templates.scenario: When debugging issues across multiple system components

prompt_templates.title: Test Failure Investigation
prompt_templates.prompt: This test [test name] is failing. Please follow the systematic debugging process to find why, then create a failing test case before fixing.

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Question
prompt_templates.prompt: I've tried three fixes for this issue and each revealed a new problem in a different place. Please use systematic debugging to determine if this is an architectural problem.
prompt_templates.scenario: When multiple fixes have failed and architecture needs questioning

output_examples.input: The login button is not working. Can you fix it?
output_examples.output: Before proposing fixes, I need to investigate the root cause. Let me gather evidence: What error appears when clicking the button? Can you share the relevant code? Has this worked before? What changed recently?

Following systematic debugging: First, I'll reproduce consistently to understand the exact conditions. Then check recent changes that could cause this, trace the data flow from request to error, and identify the root cause before proposing any fix.

output_examples.input: Fix the null pointer exception in user-service.ts line 42
output_examples.output: I'll investigate root cause first. Where does the null value originate? What called this with null? Let me trace back through the call stack to find the source, then fix at the origin rather than adding defensive code at line 42.

best_practices: Always complete root cause investigation before proposing any fix - this prevents fixing symptoms instead of causes
Gather evidence through reproduction and instrumentation rather than guessing based on symptoms
When three or more fixes have failed, question the architecture rather than attempting another fix
Create a failing test case before implementing the fix to ensure the fix actually resolves the issue
anti_patterns: Proposing fixes without completing Phase 1 root cause investigation
Trying multiple random fixes to see what works ('shotgun debugging')
Skipping the test case creation step and manually verifying instead
Adding multiple changes at once to 'save time' - this prevents isolating what actually worked

faq.question: When should I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use this skill whenever you encounter any bug, test failure, unexpected behavior, or technical issue. It is especially important under time pressure when guessing seems faster but systematic debugging actually saves time.

faq.question: Does this slow down debugging?
faq.answer: No. Systematic debugging is faster than random guessing. Random fixes have a 40% first-time success rate while systematic approaches achieve 95% success, saving hours of rework.

faq.question: What if the issue is truly environmental or external?
faq.answer: If systematic investigation reveals the issue is truly environmental, timing-dependent, or external, you have completed the process. Document what you investigated and implement appropriate handling like retries or error messages.

faq.question: How do I know when to question architecture?
faq.answer: Question architecture when you have tried 3 or more fixes and each revealed a new problem in a different place, or when fixes require massive refactoring to implement. This indicates a fundamental architectural problem, not a simple bug.

faq.question: Can this skill help with performance issues?
faq.answer: Yes. The systematic approach applies to any technical issue including performance problems. Use the same root cause investigation to find why performance is degraded before attempting optimizations.

faq.question: What supporting techniques are included?
faq.answer: This skill includes root-cause-tracing for backward call stack analysis, defense-in-depth for adding validation at multiple layers, and condition-based waiting for replacing arbitrary timeouts with proper condition polling.
